title: AI Native Software Engineer
company_stage: Growth (joint venture with Mitsubishi, powering major companies)
company_focus:
Energy intelligence platform for real-time grid and device
orchestration
description:
Job title: AI Native Software Engineer (People and Finance)
Location: London Team: People and Finance Reporting To:
People Operations & Reward Director

This role is based in London, UK and requires existing right
to work in the UK.  At this time, we are not able to offer
visa sponsorship for this role. We are committed to building
a diverse, global team and our sponsorship policy is
evaluated on a role-by-role basis. We encourage you to keep
an eye on our careers site to stay informed about future
opportunities where we are able to offer visa sponsorship.

Kaluza is the Energy Intelligence Platform, turning energy
complexity into seamless coordination. We help energy
companies overcome today’s challenges while accelerating the
shift to a clean, electrified future.

Our platform orchestrates millions of real-time decisions
across homes, devices, markets and grids. By combining
predictive algorithms with human-centred design, Kaluza
makes clean energy dependable, affordable and adaptive to
everyday life. With teams across Europe, North America, Asia
and Australia, and a joint venture with Mitsubishi
Corporation in Japan, we power leading companies including
OVO, AGL and ENGIE, as well as innovators like Volvo and
Volkswagen.

The Role

We're hiring an AI-native software engineer who will be
embedded across our People and Finance teams, helping us
reimagine how those functions operate in a world where AI
changes what's possible.

This isn't a traditional engineering role. You'll spend as
much time thinking about process, insight, security and
workflow as you will reviewing code written by the latest AI
tools. You'll start by automating manual processes and
building coherent dashboards from data that currently lives
in too many places — then you'll help us see further: how
can these functions stop being reactive and start leading
with intelligence?

What will I be doing?

- Build AI-Native Solutions: Design and deploy multi-step AI
  pipelines and agentic workflows using modern LLM APIs
  (Anthropic, OpenAI) to transform internal operations.
- Automate & Orchestrate: Identify manual, repetitive
  processes across People and Finance to build reliable
  automation—from onboarding flows to complex month-end
  reporting.
- Centralize Intelligence: Integrate data from core business
  systems (Workday, Greenhouse, Navan) into Databricks and
  BigQuery to create unified, real-time dashboards.
- Define the "Art of the Possible": Partner with functional
  leaders to challenge existing workflows and prototype
  "intelligent" ways of working before they become industry
  standards.
- Own the Roadmap: Act as a "team of one" initially, moving
  between high-level strategic conversations and hands-on
  technical implementation to deliver a long-term vision.
- Ensure Reliability & Security: Spend dedicated time ensuring
  the security and integrity of workflows, reviewing AI-
  generated code, and making high judgement "build vs. buy"
  decisions.

About You

Ideally you’ll have/be:

- Modern AI Proficiency: Strong experience building with
  modern AI/LLM APIs and the ability to manage complex data
  structures through Databricks and BigQuery.
- Full-Stack Versatility: Comfortable working across the
  stack, including backend logic, data pipelines, and
  lightweight front-end development.
- Embedded Experience: A history of working in cross-
  functional roles where you’ve translated business needs
  into technical reality.
- Strategic Communicator: Able to make AI concepts accessible
  to non-technical stakeholders and happy to define problems
  rather than just execute specs.

What will set you apart:

- System Integration Expertise: Specific experience
  integrating with HR or finance systems like Workday,
  Greenhouse, or Navan.
- Agentic Workflow Mastery: Proven success in building agents
  that handle routine questions and autonomous processes.

Kaluza Values Here at Kaluza we have five core values that
guide us as a business: Play to win, Solve the real problem,
Build trust every day, Own the outcome, Go further together.

Our Perks

- Pension Scheme
- Discretionary Bonus Scheme
- Private Medical Insurance + Virtual GP
- Life Assurance
- Access to Furthr - a Climate Action app
- Free Mortgage Advice and Eye Tests
- Perks at Work - access to thousands of retail discounts
- 5% Flex Fund - to spend on the benefits you want most
- 26 days holiday
- Flexible Bank Holidays - Giving you an additional 8 days
  which you can choose to take whenever you like
- Progressive Leave Policy - With no qualifying service
  periods, including 26 weeks full pay if you have a new
  addition to your family
- Personal Learning Budget - Upskill, learn and grow!
- Home Office Budget
- And more!

We want the best people

We’re keen to meet people from all walks of life — our view
is that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will
be. We want to build teams which represent a variety of
experiences, perspectives and skills, and we recognise
talent on the basis of merit and potential.

We understand some people may not apply for jobs unless they
tick every box. But if you're excited about joining us and
think you have some of what we're looking for, even if
you're not 100% sure, we'd still love to hear from you.

Find out more about working in Kaluza on our careers page
and LinkedIn.

You can also find our Applicant Data Protection Policy here.
responsibilities: Design and deploy multi-step AI pipelines and agentic workflows using LLM APIs to transform People and Finance operations
Identify and automate manual, repetitive processes across People and Finance teams including onboarding flows and month-end reporting
Integrate data from business systems (Workday, Greenhouse, Navan) into Databricks and BigQuery to create unified real-time dashboards
Partner with functional leaders to prototype intelligent ways of working and challenge existing workflows
Own the technical roadmap as an individual contributor, moving between strategic planning and hands-on implementation
Ensure reliability and security of workflows, review AI-generated code, and make build vs. buy decisions
use_cases: Automating HR processes such as employee onboarding flows
Automating Finance processes including complex month-end reporting
Building agentic workflows to handle routine People and Finance questions autonomously
Centralizing People and Finance data into unified real-time dashboards for insight and decision-making
Reimagining internal operations to be proactive and intelligence-led rather than reactive
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building and deploying multi-
step AI pipelines and agentic workflows using LLM APIs
(Anthropic, OpenAI). The engineer designs AI-native
solutions and builds autonomous agents for internal
operations, making this a hands-on AI building role rather
than a support role.
